real_insight: When you stall before a migration because rewriting feels like erasure, name what is actually mortal (the codebase) and what is not (the contract). Then begin.
objection: I expected 'embrace change' framing. Instead the analog is precise: a fintech payments service, nine-year Spring monolith, twelve thousand lines of Hibernate-ORM code, replacement to Go-on-Kubernetes with Postgres logical replication. The principal's correction — the Hibernate hack was always going to die; what survived nine years was POST /v1/charges, idempotency-key semantics, fraud-rule precedence, settlement reconciliation invariants — is the actual engineering content of the verse. The counter-example explicitly forecloses the misuse I would attack ('vanity migrations, framework chasing'). The verse explicitly says 'Krishna does not license arbitrary action; he licenses Arjuna's specific prescribed duty when paralysis has set in.' That couples the analog tightly to the doctrinal point and prevents the move I would use to mock it. Cannot strawman.
